modules.name: minner.exceptions
documentation:
Scrapy core exceptions

These exceptions are documented in docs/topics/exceptions.rst. Please don't add
new exceptions here without documenting them there.

classes.name: minner.exceptions.NotConfigured
documentation: Indicates a missing configuration situation
Internal

classes.name: minner.exceptions._InvalidOutput
documentation:
Indicates an invalid value has been returned by a middleware's processing method.
Internal and undocumented, it should not be raised or caught by user code.

classes.name: minner.exceptions.IgnoreRequest
documentation: Indicates a decision was made not to process a request
HTTP and crawling

classes.name: minner.exceptions.DontCloseSpider
documentation: Request the spider not to be closed yet

classes.name: minner.exceptions.CloseSpider
documentation: Raise this from callbacks to request the spider to be closed

classes.name: minner.exceptions.StopDownload
documentation:
Stop the download of the body for a given response.
The 'fail' boolean parameter indicates whether or not the resulting partial response
should be handled by the request errback. Note that 'fail' is a keyword-only argument.

classes.name: minner.exceptions.DropItem
documentation: Drop item from the item pipeline
Items

classes.name: minner.exceptions.NotSupported
documentation: Indicates a feature or method is not supported

classes.name: minner.exceptions.UsageError
documentation: To indicate a command-line usage error
Commands

classes.name: minner.exceptions.ScrapyDeprecationWarning
documentation:
Warning category for deprecated features, since the default
DeprecationWarning is silenced on Python 2.7+

classes.name: minner.exceptions.ContractFail
documentation: Error raised in case of a failing contract

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: minner.cmdline.walk_modules
documentation:
Loads a module and all its submodules from the given module path and
returns them. If *any* module throws an exception while importing, that
exception is thrown back.

For example: walk_modules('scrapy.utils')

modules.name: minner.commands
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: minner.commands.arglist_to_dict
documentation:
Convert a list of arguments like ['arg1=val1', 'arg2=val2', ...] to a
dict

methods.name: minner.commands.MinnerCommand.syntax
documentation: Command syntax (preferably one-line). Do not include command name.

methods.name: minner.commands.MinnerCommand.short_desc
documentation: A short description of the command

methods.name: minner.commands.MinnerCommand.long_desc
documentation:
A long description of the command. Return short description when not
available. It cannot contain newlines since contents will be formatted
by optparser which removes newlines and wraps text.

methods.name: minner.commands.MinnerCommand.help
documentation:
An extensive help for the command. It will be shown when using the
"help" command. It can contain newlines since no post-formatting will
be applied to its contents.

methods.name: minner.commands.MinnerCommand.add_options
documentation: Populate option parse with options available for this command

methods.name: minner.commands.MinnerCommand.run
documentation: Entry point for running commands
